title: AI automation hits insurance jobs as Allianz plans cuts
excerpt: Nearly half of respondents expect AI automation to replace a quarter of staff as Allianz Partners plans to cut up to 1,800 roles.
content_html:
GlobalDataFinancialServices

Mon, July 20, 2026 at 6:30 AM EDT 2 min read

A GlobalData poll found that nearly half of respondents believe automation will replace over 25% of their company's workforce. Meanwhile, Allianz is reportedly planning to cut 1,500–1,800 roles as the insurer expands its use of AI across the business.

A GlobalData poll conducted in Q4 2025 on Verdict and Business Trade Media International sites (which received more than 2,000 responses) found that 48.6% of respondents believe automation will replace over 25% of their company's workforce. Within this group, 25.2% expect the figure to exceed 50%. However, a separate GlobalData poll also conducted in Q4 2025 found that 44.8% of the 2,000+ respondents were not concerned that automation will replace their job.

Meanwhile, Bloomberg has reported that Allianz Partners plans to cut 1,500–1,800 roles as it expands its use of AI. The reductions—expected across several European countries—will be implemented through severance packages, early retirement schemes, and other voluntary arrangements, according to CEO Tomas Kunzmann. The move reflects a broader trend among European insurers as AI adoption accelerates.

AI is reshaping insurers' cost structures and operating models by automating high-volume, rule-based work in claims, customer service, underwriting support, fraud detection, and document processing. In the near term, this can improve speed and consistency, lower expense ratios, and help address tight labor markets, while also pushing insurers to redesign workflows, consolidate roles, and invest in data quality, modern platforms, and model governance. At the same time, it increases risk and compliance demands since regulators and customers will scrutinise fairness and transparency in pricing and claims decisions, cyber risk grows, and over-reliance on automation can create resilience issues if systems fail.

Over time, insurers that combine AI with strong controls and workforce upskilling are more likely to improve risk selection, launch more tailored products, and deliver faster service. In contrast, providers that fall behind may face weaker customer retention and margin pressure.

"AI automation hits insurance jobs as Allianz plans cuts" was originally created and published by Life Insurance International, a GlobalData owned brand.

sentences.en: "AI automation hits insurance jobs as Allianz plans cuts" was originally created and published by Life Insurance International, a GlobalData owned brand.
sentences.zh: 《安联计划裁员，人工智能自动化冲击保险业就业岗位》最初由 GlobalData 旗下品牌 Life Insurance International 创建和发布。

content_html_zh:
全球数据金融服务公司

2026 年 7 月 20 日星期一，美国东部时间上午 6:30 阅读 2 分钟

GlobalData 的一项民意调查发现，近一半的受访者认为自动化将取代公司 25% 以上的员工。 与此同时，据报道，随着保险公司在整个业务范围内扩大人工智能的使用，安联保险计划裁员 1,500-1,800 名。

GlobalData 于 2025 年第四季度在 Verdict 和 Business Trade Media International 网站上进行的一项民意调查（收到了 2,000 多份回复）发现，48.6% 的受访者认为自动化将取代公司 25% 以上的员工。 在这一群体中，25.2%的人预计这一数字将超过50%。 然而，GlobalData 在 2025 年第四季度进行的另一项民意调查发现，2000 多名受访者中，有 44.8% 的人并不担心自动化会取代他们的工作。

与此同时，彭博社报道称，Allianz Partners 计划在扩大人工智能应用的同时裁减 1,500-1,800 名员工。 首席执行官托马斯·昆兹曼 (Tomas Kunzmann) 表示，预计在几个欧洲国家进行的裁员将通过遣散费、提前退休计划和其他自愿安排来实施。 此举反映了随着人工智能采用加速，欧洲保险公司的更广泛趋势。

人工智能正在通过自动化大批量、基于规则的索赔、客户服务、承保支持、欺诈检测和文档处理工作，重塑保险公司的成本结构和运营模式。 在短期内，这可以提高速度和一致性，降低费用比率，并有助于解决劳动力市场紧张的问题，同时也推动保险公司重新设计工作流程，整合角色，并投资于数据质量、现代平台和模型治理。 与此同时，它增加了风险和合规性要求，因为监管机构和客户将审查定价和索赔决策的公平性和透明度，网络风险增加，并且如果系统出现故障，过度依赖自动化可能会产生弹性问题。

随着时间的推移，将人工智能与强大的控制和员工技能提升相结合的保险公司更有可能改善风险选择、推出更量身定制的产品并提供更快的服务。 相比之下，落后的供应商可能面临较弱的客户保留率和利润压力。

《安联计划裁员，人工智能自动化冲击保险业就业岗位》最初由 GlobalData 旗下品牌 Life Insurance International 创建和发布。
